description: A horizontal designed graphic in the same DHS '#PetPreparedness' employee-feature template as the Indy/Deborah card. Cream/beige background with a faint repeating pattern of dog-bone, fish, and paw-print line art. Top-left brown rounded banner with white serif text 'Pet Preparedness Tips.' Dark sans-serif body text reads: 'Charley's summer ready kit includes a lot of fly and tick gear; fly sheets, fly boots, fly mask, and being sprayed daily with fly spray. She also gets sunscreen on the little white of her nose on days she isn't wearing a fly mask that covers it. Charley is on joint supplements and ulcer preventatives, and gets electrolytes daily in her grain to ensure she is drinking enough on hot summer days.' On the right is a rounded color photograph of a woman in a baseball cap, T-shirt and jeans riding a brown American Paint Horse with white markings through a green grassy field. Below the photo, bold black serif text reads 'Bridgett & Charley,' with a teal accent line, 'TSA' and 'Lead Transportation Security Officer.' The circular DHS seal is bottom-right. A humanizing public-affairs graphic featuring a TSA employee and her horse to promote pet/animal summer-safety preparedness; aimed at the general public and pet/horse owners to build agency goodwill.
notable_text: Pet Preparedness Tips; Bridgett & Charley; TSA; Lead Transportation Security Officer
